Entries.UsedFor: Establishing the GraphQL endpoint URL and REST utility endpoint base URL for the Path LMS Reporting API
Entries.Excerpt: Base URL: https://data-api.pathlms.com. GraphQL Query Endpoint: POST {{baseURL}}/graphql

Entries.UsedFor: Establishing the token endpoint URL for the two-step auth flow
Establishing the two-step-token auth flow: POST x-www-form-urlencoded {applicationId, applicationSecret} to /api/v1/getToken returns a 12-hour bearer token
Establishing the exact Authorization header format for all GraphQL requests
Establishing that token payload uses x-www-form-urlencoded content type
Entries.Excerpt: Note: Your request should contain a x-www-form-urlencoded payload with an applicationId and an applicationSecret key.

Entries.UsedFor: Establishing the token request parameter names: applicationId and applicationSecret
Establishing token lifetime of 12 hours with no refresh token — must re-mint on expiry
Entries.Excerpt: Note: The bearer token that is returned is valid for 12 hours from the time you generate the token. Once the token has expired, you will need to generate a new token.

Entries.UsedFor: Establishing offset/limit pagination as the vendor-wide pagination model
Entries.Excerpt: limit: Defines how many records are returned. offset: Defines an offset value. For example, if you define offset as 50 and limit as 50, the response will return records 51-101.

Entries.UsedFor: Confirming zero mutations and zero subscriptions in the SDL — pull-only API, no write capability
Entries.Excerpt: SpectaQL HTML at https://data-api.pathlms.com/ renders the full GraphQL SDL: 17 Query-type fields, 0 mutation fields, 0 subscription fields. Confirmed by schema_extracted.json: mutationCount=0, subscriptionCount=0.

Entries.UsedFor: Confirming DefaultSyncDirection=Pull — the Reporting API is read-only
Entries.Excerpt: The Path LMS Reporting API is a GraphQL-based API designed to provide efficient data processing and flexible querying capabilities. [No mutations documented]

Entries.UsedFor: Confirming schema statistics: 17 queries (1 utility root + 16 operative), 93 object types, 0 mutations, 0 subscriptions. Source: SpectaQL HTML extraction.

Establishing universalPK hint: 'id' field present as primary identifier on all concrete types with identity fields (Assessment, Assignment, Course, Webinar, Order, User, BaseTeam, etc.) — >95% coverage across named object types
Entries.Excerpt: Assessment.id, Assignment.id, Certificate.id, Course.id, Webinar.id, Order.id, User.id, BaseTeam.id, BaseAccount.id — 'id' field consistent across all concrete types in the SDL. userId appears on result/report types referencing the User entity.

Entries.UsedFor: GAP DOCUMENTED: No rate limit documentation found in any credential-free source. BatchMaxRequestCount and BatchRequestWaitTime set to -1 (unknown). Verify with Blue Sky eLearn support.
Entries.Excerpt: No rate limit documentation found in SpectaQL docs page at data-api.pathlms.com/. No X-RateLimit-* headers mentioned. Gap documented; -1 = unknown.

Entries.UsedFor: Establishing IncrementalSyncCapability=false at the integration level. The SDL exposes startDate/endDate filter arguments but these are event-date filters, not modification-time watermarks. No updatedAt/modifiedSince cursor documented anywhere in the SDL.
Entries.Excerpt: startDate: Start date to filter report (String YYYY-MM-DD). endDate: End date to filter report (String YYYY-MM-DD). These filter by event activity date, not by record modification time. No updatedSince or modifiedAfter parameter documented in any query in the SDL.

Entries.UsedFor: Establishing GraphQL nested-container query model: account and teams are L1 scope containers; all sub-reports are accessed as fields within them with per-field arguments
Entries.Excerpt: This query will allow you access to account data. Users can call this query with a valid authorization access token. All queries under this top level query will report data with account data. [Account type has 63 sub-fields, all with offset/limit pagination arguments]

Entries.UsedFor: Documenting the out-of-scope admin/content API at pathlms.com/api/v1 — separate REST API for course/user/enrollment management, NOT part of the Reporting API connector scope
Entries.Excerpt: The Path LMS Reporting API is distinct from the admin API at pathlms.com/api/v1. The 'OutOfScopeObjectFamilies' list captures the admin/content object surface (users, courses, enrollments, etc.) and the task-provided scope decision that these are deferred in favor of the GraphQL Reporting API surface.

Entries.UsedFor: Setting OutOfScopeObjectFamilies to the EXACT spec-mandated list from the workflow task prompt. These 19 items are the admin/content API object families in the broader Path LMS product (pathlms.com/api/v1) that are not part of the Reporting GraphQL API surface (data-api.pathlms.com/graphql). Scope decision: defer to Reporting-only connector for analytics use case.
Entries.Excerpt: Spec-mandated list (workflow task prompt, exact): ["users","courses","course_presentations","enrollments","assignments","assessments","surveys","certificates","certified_credits","credits","events","in_person_events","live_web_events","event_registrations","live_web_event_registrations","scorm_content","transactions","badges"]. These object families exist in the Path LMS admin/content REST API (pathlms.com/api/v1) but are not exposed via the Reporting GraphQL API. OutOfScopeReason: "MJ clients consume Path LMS analytics via the GraphQL Reporting API (data-api.pathlms.com/graphql); the broader Path LMS admin/content product + the CSV-export egress are real but not the reachable/useful object surface for this use case. Reporting query set modelled deeply from the SpectaQL SDL; the rest deferred-with-reason."

Entries.UsedFor: Establishing the public credential-free schema URL. The SpectaQL-rendered page at https://data-api.pathlms.com/ embeds the complete GraphQL SDL and is the schema-of-record for the Path LMS Reporting API connector. Live GraphQL introspection at /graphql is auth-gated (JWT required at Apollo context()). The SpectaQL page is the sole credential-free, machine-parseable schema surface.
Entries.Excerpt: SpectaQL docs page at https://data-api.pathlms.com/ — HTTP 200, 1,196,184 bytes, credential-free. Embeds full GraphQL SDL: 93 OBJECT types, 17 Query operations, 0 mutations, 0 subscriptions. Live /graphql endpoint confirms auth gate via HTTP 500 "Context creation failed: JsonWebTokenError: jwt must be provided".

Entries.UsedFor: Establishing the TotalCount companion-field pattern on Account and Team types. The SpectaQL SDL documents <subReportName>TotalCount: Int! fields alongside each paginated sub-report array on Account (e.g. usersTotalCount, coursesTotalCount, ordersTotalCount, assessmentsTotalCount, etc.) and Team. The connector reads these companion fields to compute total pages without an extra round-trip.
Entries.Excerpt: From SpectaQL Account type definition: "usersTotalCount: Int!" — "This query will return a total count of users for an account." "coursesTotalCount: Int!", "assessmentsTotalCount: Int!", "assignmentsTotalCount: Int!", "certificatesTotalCount: Int!", "certifiedCreditsTotalCount: Int!", "coursePresentationsTotalCount: Int!", "eventsTotalCount: Int!", "inPersonEventsTotalCount: Int!", "liveWebEventsTotalCount: Int!", "scormTotalCount: Int!", "surveysTotalCount: Int!", "ordersTotalCount: Int!", "orderItemsTotalCount: Int!", "refundsTotalCount: Int!", "refundsDetailsTotalCount: Int!", "salesByCategoryTotalCount: Int!", "salesByUserTotalCount: Int!", "salesByBundleTotalCount: Int!", "commerceSalesByContentTotalCount: Int!", "couponsTotalCount: Int!", "discountsTotalCount: Int!", "externalActivityTotalCount: Int!" — 24 TotalCount companion fields documented in Account type. Pattern: <subReportName>TotalCount.
